request.message: find me shear resistancr of ipe400

meta.rationale: Heuristic tool selection for member resistance query.

assumptions: Assumed steel grade S355 as it was not specified.
Assumed partial safety factor gamma_M0 = 1.0 as per standard Eurocode 3 defaults.

iteration.query: find me shear resistancr of ipe400
find me shear resistancr of ipe400

top_clauses.title: Combined shear force, axial force and bending moment

response.answer:
For **IPE400** in **S355** steel, the design shear resistance is **VRd = 865.95 kN**. This follows EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 5.5.2(1) (Classification of cross-sections) with supporting classification guidance from Cl. Table 5.2 (Width-to-thickness limits).
Section Classification detailed results **Inputs** | Input | Value | | --- | --- | | section name | IPE400 | | h (mm) | 400 mm | | b (mm) | 180 mm | | tw (mm) | 8.60 mm | | tf (mm) | 13.50 mm | | fy mpa | 355 MPa | **Outputs** | Output | Value | | --- | --- | | web class | **4** | | flange class | **1** | | governing class | **4** | **Notes** - This MVP uses simplified threshold values aligned to EC3 structure for demonstrative chaining.
Member Resistance detailed results **Inputs** | Input | Value | | --- | --- | | section name | IPE400 | | steel grade | S355 | | fy mpa | 355 MPa | | section class | 4 | | γM0 | 1 | | w used (cm³) | 1156 cm³ | | area (cm²) | 84.50 cm² | | av z (cm²) | 42.25 cm² | **Outputs** | Output | Value | | --- | --- | | MRd (kNm) | **410.38 kNm** | | NRd (kN) | **2999.75 kN** | | VRd (kN) | **865.95 kN** | | bending formula basis | **MRd = W * fy / γM0** | | axial formula basis | **NRd = A * fy / γM0** | | shear formula basis | **VRd = A_v * fy / (sqrt(3) * γM0)** | **Notes** - Member buckling and lateral torsional buckling are outside this MVP placeholder calculator.
Assumptions used - Assumed steel grade S355 as it was not specified. - Assumed partial safety factor γM0 = 1.0 as per standard Eurocode 3 defaults.
--- **References:** 1. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 5.5.2(1) — Classification of cross-sections 2. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. Table 5.2 — Width-to-thickness limits 3. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.5(1) — Bending resistance 4. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.4(1) — Compression resistance 5. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.6(1) — Shear resistance
assumptions: Assumed steel grade S355 as it was not specified.
Assumed partial safety factor gamma_M0 = 1.0 as per standard Eurocode 3 defaults.

response.answer:
For **IPE300** in **S355** steel, the design bending resistance is **MRd = 223.08 kNm**. Grounded summary: The response is based only on retrieved EC3 clauses and tool outputs. This follows EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.5(1) (Bending resistance) with supporting classification guidance from Cl. 5.5.2(1) (Classification of cross-sections).

Assumptions used - γM0 = 1.00 (standard EC3 value).

For **IPE300** in **S355** steel, the design bending resistance is **MRd = 223.08 kNm**. Grounded summary: The response is based only on retrieved EC3 clauses and tool outputs. This follows EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.5(1) (Bending resistance) with supporting classification guidance from Cl. 5.5.2(1) (Classification of cross-sections).
IPE Moment Resistance detailed results **Inputs** | Input | Value | | --- | --- | | section name | IPE300 | | steel grade | S355 | | fy mpa | 355 MPa | | section class | 2 | | γM0 | 1 | **Outputs** | Output | Value | | --- | --- | | MRd (kNm) | **223.08 kNm** | | formula | **MRd = W * fy / γM0** | | section class interpretation | **Plastic resistance basis** | **Notes** - This tool focuses on cross-section moment resistance only (no buckling/LTB checks). - IPE profile source used: fallback_embedded.

--- **References:** 1. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.5(1) — Bending resistance 2. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 5.5.2(1) — Classification of cross-sections **Retrieved Clauses (JSON):** ```json { "doc_id": "ec3.en1993-1-3.2006", "standard": "EN 1993-1-3", "clause_id": "6.2.5", "clause_title": "Bending and axial compression", "pointer": "en_1993_1_3_2006#/6.2.5", "text": "(1) The interaction between axial force and bending moment may be obtained from a second-order analysis of the member as specified in EN 1993-1-1, based on the properties of the effective cross-section obtained from Section 5.5. See also 5.3.\n\n(2) As an alternative the interaction formula $N_{Ed}/N_{b,Rd}$ may be used\n\n$$\\left( \\frac{N_{Ed}}{N_{b,Rd}} \\right)^{0.8} + \\left( \\frac{M_{Ed}}{M_{b,Rd}} \\right)^{0.8} \\leq 1.0$$\n\n...(6.36)\n\nwhere $N_{b,Rd}$ is the design buckling resistance of a compression member according to 6.2.2 (flexural, torsional or torsional-flexural buckling) and $M_{b,Rd}$ is the design bending moment resistance according to 6.2.4 and $M_{Ed}$ includes the effects of shift of neutral axis, if relevant." } ```
assumptions: γ_M0 = 1.00 (standard EC3 value).

response.answer:
For **IPE300** in **S355** steel, the design bending resistance is **MRd = 223.08 kNm**. Grounded summary: The response is based only on retrieved EC3 clauses and tool outputs. This follows EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.5(1) (Bending resistance) with supporting classification guidance from Cl. 5.5.2(1) (Classification of cross-sections).
IPE Moment Resistance detailed results **Inputs** | Input | Value | | --- | --- | | section name | IPE300 | | steel grade | S355 | | fy mpa | 355 MPa | | section class | 2 | | γM0 | 1 | **Outputs** | Output | Value | | --- | --- | | MRd (kNm) | **223.08 kNm** | | formula | **MRd = W * fy / γM0** | | section class interpretation | **Plastic resistance basis** | **Notes** - This tool focuses on cross-section moment resistance only (no buckling/LTB checks). - IPE profile source used: fallback_embedded.
Assumptions used - γM0 = 1.00 (standard EC3 value).
--- **References:** 1. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 6.2.5(1) — Bending resistance 2. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 5.5.2(1) — Classification of cross-sections **Retrieved Clauses (JSON):** ```json { "doc_id": "ec3.en1993-1-3.2006", "standard": "EN 1993-1-3", "clause_id": "6.2.5", "clause_title": "Bending and axial compression", "pointer": "en_1993_1_3_2006#/6.2.5", "text": "(1) The interaction between axial force and bending moment may be obtained from a second-order analysis of the member as specified in EN 1993-1-1, based on the properties of the effective cross-section obtained from Section 5.5. See also 5.3.\n\n(2) As an alternative the interaction formula $N_{Ed}/N_{b,Rd}$ may be used\n\n$$\\left( \\frac{N_{Ed}}{N_{b,Rd}} \\right)^{0.8} + \\left( \\frac{M_{Ed}}{M_{b,Rd}} \\right)^{0.8} \\leq 1.0$$\n\n...(6.36)\n\nwhere $N_{b,Rd}$ is the design buckling resistance of a compression member according to 6.2.2 (flexural, torsional or torsional-flexural buckling) and $M_{b,Rd}$ is the design bending moment resistance according to 6.2.4 and $M_{Ed}$ includes the effects of shift of neutral axis, if relevant." } ```
assumptions: γ_M0 = 1.00 (standard EC3 value).

response.answer:
For **IPE300** in **S355** steel, the design bending resistance is **MRd = 197.74 kNm**. This follows EN 1993-1-1, Cl. 5.5.2(1) (Classification of cross-sections) with supporting classification guidance from Cl. Table 5.2 (Width-to-thickness limits).
Section Classification detailed results | Input | Value | | --- | --- | | section name | IPE300 | | h (mm) | 300 mm | | b (mm) | 150 mm | | tw (mm) | 7.10 mm | | tf (mm) | 10.70 mm | | fy mpa | 355 MPa | | Output | Value | | --- | --- | | web class | **4** | | flange class | **1** | | governing class | **4** | **Notes** - This MVP uses simplified threshold values aligned to EC3 structure for demonstrative chaining.
Member Resistance detailed results | Input | Value | | --- | --- | | section name | IPE300 | | steel grade | S355 | | fy mpa | 355 MPa | | section class | 4 | | γM0 | 1 | | w used (cm³) | 557 cm³ | | area (cm²) | 53.80 cm² | | av z (cm²) | 27.50 cm² | | Output | Value | | --- | --- | | MRd (kNm) | **197.74 kNm** | | NRd (kN) | **1909.90 kN** | | VRd (kN) | **563.64 kN** | | bending formula basis | **MRd = W * fy / γM0** | | axial formula basis | **NRd = A * fy / γM0** | | shear formula basis | **VRd = A_v * fy / (sqrt(3) * γM0)** | **Notes** - Member buckling and lateral torsional buckling are outside this MVP placeholder calculator.
Assumptions used - Assumed γM0 = 1.0 as the standard partial safety factor for cross-section resistance. - Assumed section geometry and properties will be retrieved from the built-in library using the section_name.

request.message: Simply supported beam, 6m span, 15 kN/m UDL. What is the maximum bending moment and deflection?

meta.rationale: The user is asking for basic structural analysis results (maximum bending moment and deflection) for a simply supported beam with a uniformly distributed load. This is a direct calculation task suited for the simple_beam_calculator tool. No Eurocode clause retrieval is necessary for basic statics, though the agent may need to prompt the user for section properties (E and I) to complete the deflection calculation.

assumptions: Assumed Young's modulus E = 210 GPa for steel.
Assumed default section IPE300 (I_y = 8356 cm^4) to calculate deflection, as no section was specified by the user.

response.answer:
the design result is **M max = 67.50 kNm**. This is checked against EN 1993-1-1, Cl. simply_supported_beam (Simply supported beam under UDL — standard formulae).
Simple Beam Calculator detailed results
ParameterValue
load typeudl
span (m)6 m
w kN per (m)15 kN
E (GPa)210 GPa
I (cm⁴)8356 cm⁴
OutputValue
M max (kNm)67.50 kNm
V max (kN)45 kN
R A (kN)45 kN
R B (kN)45 kN
delta max (mm)14.43 mm
**Notes** - Standard beam theory (Euler-Bernoulli). Shear deformation neglected.
Assumptions used - Assumed Young's modulus E = 210 GPa for steel. - Assumed default section IPE300 (I_y = 8356 cm^4) to calculate deflection, as no section was specified by the user.
--- **References:** 1. EN 1993-1-1, Cl. simply_supported_beam — Simply supported beam under UDL — standard formulae

response.answer:
This follows EN 1993-1-3, Ref. Table 10.5 (Reaction force at support for simply supported beam) with supporting reference to EN 1993-1-6, Ref. B.5.2 (Local distributed load, simply supported boundary). This follows EN 1993-1-3, Ref. Table 10.5 (Reaction force at support for simply supported beam) with supporting reference to EN 1993-1-6, Ref. B.5.2 (Local distributed load, simply supported boundary). --- **References:**
1. EN 1993-1-3, Ref. Table 10.5 — Reaction force at support for simply supported beam

| Beam and loading | Reaction force on bottom flange $R_1$ | Reaction force on top flange $R_2$ |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Z-beam, gravity loading | $(1-\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ | $(1+\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ |
| Z-beam, uplift loading | $-(1-\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ | $-(1+\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ |
| C-beam, gravity loading | $(1-\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ | $-(1-\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ |
| C-beam, uplift loading | $-(1-\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ | $(1-\xi)k_hq_{Ed}L/2$ |

(3) The factor $\zeta$ may be taken as $\zeta = 1 - \sqrt[3]{\kappa_R^2}$ where $\kappa_R$ is the correction factor given in Table 10.1, and the factor $\xi$ may be taken as $\xi = 1,5\zeta$.

2. EN 1993-1-6, Ref. B.5.2 — Local distributed load, simply supported boundary

Uniform pressure $p_n$ on circular patch of radius $b$
$$F = p_n \pi b^2$$

with
$$K = 1,0 + 1,10 \frac{b}{r} + 1,15 \left( \frac{b}{r} \right)^4 \text{ or } K = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \cdot \frac{b}{t}$$
whichever is the lesser

3. EN 1993-1-6, Ref. B.5.1 — Uniform load, simply supported boundary

$$p_{n,R} = 1,625 \left( \frac{t}{r} \right)^2 fy$$

4. EN 1993-1-6, Ref. C.6.1 — Plate with simply supported boundary: uniform load

$$w = 0.696 \frac{p_n r^4}{Et^3}$$

$$\text{max. } \sigma_{xb} = 1.238 p_n \left( \frac{r}{t} \right)^2$$

$$\text{max. } \sigma_{\theta b} = 1.238 p_n \left( \frac{r}{t} \right)^2$$

$$p_{n,y} = 0.808 \left( \frac{t}{r} \right)^2 fy$$

5. EN 1993-1-6, Ref. C.6.2 — Plate with local distributed load: simply supported boundary

Uniform pressure $p_n$ on circular patch of radius $b$
$$F = p_n \pi b^2$$

$$b < 0.2 r$$

$$w = 0.606 \frac{Fr^2}{Et^3}$$

$$\text{max. } \sigma_{xb} = \text{max. } \sigma_{\theta b} = 0.621 \frac{F}{t^2} \left( \ln \frac{b}{r} + 0.769 \right)$$

$$F_y = 1.611 \frac{t^2}{\left( \ln \frac{b}{r} + 0.769 \right) fy}$$

6. EN 1993-1-3, Ref. A.2.2 — Single span test

(1) A test set-up equivalent to that shown in figure A.2 may be used to determine the midspan moment resistance (in the absence of shear force) and the effective flexural stiffness.

(2) The span should be chosen such that the test results represent the moment resistance of the sheet.

(3) The moment resistance should be determined from the test result.

(4) The flexural stiffness should be determined from a plot of the load-deflection behaviour.

response.answer:
the design result is **M max = 67.50 kNm**. This is checked against EN 1993-1-1, Cl. simply_supported_beam (Simply supported beam under UDL — standard formulae).
